public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator.
appearance: Nora Mere carries the marks of quiet-room mediator in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quiet-room mediator in and around Velvet Pinnacle Hall, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Velvet Pinnacle Hall while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Nora Mere can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Quiet-Room Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Nora Mere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Quiet-Room Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Nora Mere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Nora Mere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Nora Mere is Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Quiet-Room Mediator.
social_texture: Nora Mere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Velvet Pinnacle Hall, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Nora touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Nora Mere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Velvet Pinnacle Hall: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
